headline: What Are the Biggest Mistakes to Avoid in a Bathroom Remodel?
articleBody:
We’ve seen it all, and it’s taught us that a successful bathroom remodel is all about avoiding a few common pitfalls. Taking the time to get things right at the start saves so many headaches down the road.

First, don’t rush the planning phase. It’s tempting to want to get started right away, but making design decisions without a complete plan almost always leads to delays and rework. You need everything from the tile to the fixtures selected before the first hammer swings. Next, don’t just hire the cheapest labor for complex jobs. Waterproofing a shower or handling plumbing and electrical work requires real skill. Skimping here can lead to disastrous leaks and code violations that are expensive to fix later.

Another misstep is trying to move all the plumbing unnecessarily. If your budget is a concern, a smart designer can often create a fantastic new layout while keeping the main plumbing stacks in place, which significantly reduces cost and complexity. Finally, make sure you have a detailed contract that spells everything out. A vague agreement is a recipe for conflict.

Ready to get started the right way? City Dweller, LLC has been helping Bay Area homeowners navigate bathroom remodels since 2013. Give us a call for a consultation.
